title: Fully Funded UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland | €25,000 Annual Stipend for International and EU Students
summary: Applications are now officially open for the highly prestigious School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 at University College Cork (UCC) in Ireland. This fully funded doctoral scholarship offers outstanding students the opportunity to pursue advanced legal research at one of Ireland’s leading universities while receiving comprehensive financial support. The scholarship is open to both EU and non-EU applicants and provides: Full tuition fee coverage An annual stipend of EUR 25,000 Funding support for up to four years This opportunity is designed for ambitious scholars who are passionate about legal research, academic excellence, and contributing meaningful knowledge to the field of law. With access to internationally recognised supervisors, a vibrant doctoral research community, and tailored academic support systems, the UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship represents an exceptional pathway for aspiring legal researchers and academics. About University College Cork (UCC) University College Cork (UCC) is one of Ireland’s leading higher education institutions, internationally recognised for academic excellence, research innovation, and student development. The School of Law at UCC is known for: High-quality legal research Internationally respected faculty Strong doctoral training Interdisciplinary legal scholarship Supportive academic environment The university offers doctoral researchers access to: Expert supervision Research training Academic resources Networking opportunities Professional development activities Students joining the programme become part of a thriving research community committed to advancing legal scholarship and public impact. Overview of the UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 The School of Law PhD Scholarship is offered to candidates commencing PhD studies in Autumn 2026. The scholarship supports students pursuing doctoral research in any field of law and aims to attract highly talented researchers with strong academic backgrounds and research potential. This fully funded scholarship allows recipients to focus on high-level research without the burden of tuition costs or financial constraints. Scholarship Benefits The UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 provides substantial financial and academic support to successful candidates. 1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage The scholarship fully covers tuition fees for: EU applicants Non-EU applicants This makes the programme accessible to international students from around the world. 2. Annual Stipend of EUR 25,000 Recipients will receive: EUR 25,000 per year Funding support for a maximum of four years The stipend is intended to help cover living expenses and allow students to focus on their research activities. 3. Access to Internationally Recognised Supervisors PhD scholars at UCC benefit from supervision by experts who are internationally recognised in their respective legal fields. This provides students with: High-level academic mentorship Research guidance Professional development support Opportunities for scholarly collaboration 4. Vibrant Doctoral Research Community Students become part of a dynamic and supportive doctoral environment with access to: Research seminars Workshops Academic conferences Collaborative research opportunities Tailored doctoral support systems 5. Potential Teaching and Tutoring Opportunities The university notes that separately remunerated opportunities in: Lecturing Tutoring may also become available during the PhD programme. These experiences can help doctoral students build teaching portfolios and strengthen future academic career prospects. Research Areas One of the major strengths of this scholarship is its flexibility regarding research topics. The scholarship is open to candidates in: Any field of law Applicants are encouraged to: Develop a strong research proposal Identify potential supervisors Contact supervisors before applying This early engagement can help applicants strengthen their applications and align research interests with faculty expertise. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must carefully review the scholarship requirements before applying. Minimum Academic Requirements Candidates must have: A First-Class honours degree or a high 2.1 honours undergraduate degree in law (or equivalent) In addition, applicants should also possess: A Master’s degree in law or a related field OR Extensive and relevant professional experience demonstrating capacity for independent research and academic writing Ideal Candidates for the Scholarship The scholarship is best suited for applicants who: Have strong academic records Demonstrate research potential Possess analytical and writing skills Are passionate about legal scholarship Intend to contribute to legal research and policy development Applicants with interdisciplinary legal interests may also find this opportunity highly valuable. Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion, and Equity The School of Law at UCC strongly encourages applications from: Under-represented groups in doctoral education Individuals from diverse backgrounds Candidates from fields where representation is limited The institution has expressed a strong commitment to: Diversity Inclusion Equity Equal opportunity in higher education and research This creates a welcoming and supportive environment for scholars from different countries, backgrounds, and experiences. Why You Should Apply Pursuing a fully funded PhD scholarship at an internationally respected university can significantly enhance your academic and professional future. Benefits of this opportunity include: Earning a doctorate from a leading Irish university Conducting advanced legal research Building international academic networks Receiving generous financial support Working with leading legal scholars Gaining teaching and research experience Strengthening future career opportunities in academia, policy, law, and international organisations Application Process Interested applicants are advised to: Prepare a strong research proposal Review all scholarship requirements carefully Contact potential supervisors Complete the official application process Applicants should ensure that all required documents and information are submitted before the deadline. Read the requirements and apply here: School of Law PhD Scholarship Requirements and Application Form 2026 Application Deadline Deadline: 19 June 2026 at 5:00 PM GMT Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early. Final Thoughts The UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring legal scholars seeking fully funded doctoral study in Ireland. With full tuition coverage, a generous annual stipend, expert supervision, and access to a vibrant academic community, the programme provides an excellent platform for conducting impactful legal research at an internationally respected institution. For students who are passionate about law, policy, legal reform, and academic research, this scholarship could become a transformative step toward a successful international academic and professional career. For more information, visit the official website. Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Fully Funded UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland | €25,000 Annual Stipend for International and EU Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Media Foundation for West Africa Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women 2026 in Ghana
summary: Investigative journalism remains one of the most powerful tools for promoting accountability, exposing corruption, combating disinformation, and strengthening democratic governance. However, women continue to be underrepresented in investigative and accountability journalism across many African countries, including Ghana. To bridge this gap and empower the next generation of female investigative journalists, the Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA), with support from the European Union through International Media Support, has announced the 2026 Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women in Ghana. This fully funded 12-month hybrid mentorship programme is designed to equip young Ghanaian women with practical investigative journalism skills, newsroom exposure, professional mentorship, and editorial support to become strong voices in accountability journalism. The initiative forms part of the African Media Integrity and Resilience Programme being implemented by the MFWA. About the Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme The programme is targeted at female journalism graduates, early-career journalists, and young professionals who are passionate about governance reporting, investigative journalism, ethical media practice, and countering disinformation. Selected participants will undergo a structured mentorship experience that combines residential training, virtual coaching, and newsroom attachment opportunities. The programme aims to nurture women journalists who can contribute meaningfully to public accountability, transparency, and democratic development in Ghana. Structure of the Programme The mentorship programme will run from July 2026 to June 2027 and will follow a hybrid format. 1. Two Months Residential Training Participants will begin with an intensive two-month in-person residential training programme. Accommodation will be fully provided at a designated training location. During this phase, fellows will receive hands-on training in: Investigative journalism techniques Governance and policy reporting Leadership development Mobile journalism Fact-checking and verification Multimedia storytelling Digital content production Women’s safety in journalism Ethical and accountability reporting 2. Ten Months Virtual Mentorship Following the residential component, participants will continue with ten months of virtual mentorship and professional coaching. This stage will connect fellows with experienced female editors, investigative journalists, and governance experts who will guide participants throughout the programme. The mentorship phase is designed to help fellows: Strengthen investigative reporting skills Develop editorial judgment Build professional confidence Improve storytelling techniques Navigate challenges in journalism careers Expand professional networks 3. Six-Month Newsroom Attachment Within the mentorship period, participants will also complete a six-month newsroom attachment with The Fourth Estate, the investigative journalism project of the MFWA. This practical newsroom experience will expose fellows to real investigative reporting projects and editorial processes. Benefits of the Programme Successful applicants will enjoy several professional and financial benefits throughout the mentorship programme. Key Benefits Include: Intensive investigative journalism training Professional mentorship from experienced female editors Coaching from governance experts Financial support for investigative story production Editorial support for published stories Opportunity to produce at least five investigative stories Six-month newsroom attachment with The Fourth Estate Monthly stipend Certificate of honour upon successful completion Expanded professional journalism network Enhanced digital and multimedia storytelling skills Who Can Apply? The programme is open to: Female Ghanaian journalism graduates Early-career journalists Young professionals passionate about accountability journalism Applicants must also be willing to fully participate in the programme from July 2026 to June 2027. The organisers particularly encourage applications from candidates residing in: Greater Accra Region Eastern Region Central Region Ashanti Region Bono East Region Savannah Region Northern Region Upper West Region Required Application Documents Interested applicants must submit the following documents: A motivation letter (maximum 300 words) explaining why they should be considered for the programme Links to two published works: News articles for journalists Blog posts for other professionals Letter of consent from employer (required for staff journalists) Updated CV with two referees How to Apply Applicants should send all required documents via email to: applications@mfwa.org Email Subject Line: “Application for Female IJ Mentorship” Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all requirements before submission to ensure their applications are complete. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Media Foundation for West Africa Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women is: May 27, 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so interested candidates are encouraged to apply early. Why This Programme Matters The underrepresentation of women in investigative journalism continues to limit diversity in newsrooms and public discourse. Programmes such as this mentorship initiative are critical in creating opportunities for women to build successful careers in investigative reporting and governance journalism. By equipping young women with investigative skills, mentorship, and newsroom experience, the programme contributes to stronger media accountability systems and more inclusive journalism in Ghana. This opportunity is particularly valuable for aspiring investigative journalists seeking practical exposure, professional guidance, and long-term career development in media and governance reporting. For enquiries, applicants may contact Josephine Badu-Nyarko via: Phone: 0547043404 Email: josephine@mfwa.org For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Media Foundation for West Africa Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women 2026 in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 in New York (Remote): Fully Remote International Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals
summary: The United Nations Children s Fund is now accepting applications for the Youth Engagement Internship under the Youth Partnerships and Programmes Secretariat of Generation Unlimited . This highly competitive international internship opportunity offers young professionals and graduate students the chance to contribute to global youth empowerment initiatives while working remotely with one of the world’s most respected humanitarian organizations. The internship is hosted under UNICEF’s Global Programme Division and is based in New York Headquarters (NYHQ), although the role will be carried out remotely on a full-time basis for a duration of 26 weeks. UNICEF is recruiting two interns to support key areas within Generation Unlimited’s global youth engagement and innovation ecosystem. The opportunity is ideal for individuals passionate about youth development, entrepreneurship, international development, education, policy, and social impact. Applications close on 08 June 2026 (Eastern Daylight Time). About UNICEF United Nations Children s Fund operates in more than 190 countries and territories worldwide, working to protect children’s rights, improve access to education and healthcare, respond to emergencies, and support young people in reaching their full potential. UNICEF’s mission centers on ensuring every child survives, thrives, learns, and is protected from harm. The organization works closely with governments, communities, civil society organizations, and international partners to create sustainable change for children and youth globally. The organization is recognized internationally for its commitment to: Child protection Education Health and nutrition Youth empowerment Emergency response Social inclusion Gender equality Sustainable development UNICEF also places strong emphasis on diversity, inclusion, innovation, and youth participation in global decision-making processes. What is Generation Unlimited (GenU)? Generation Unlimited is a global partnership initiative launched by UNICEF alongside public and private sector organizations. The initiative focuses on expanding opportunities for young people aged 10–24 by helping them access: Education Skills training Employment Entrepreneurship opportunities Digital inclusion Social participation Generation Unlimited aims to ensure that every young person can transition successfully from learning to earning by the year 2030. The initiative particularly prioritizes vulnerable and marginalized youth groups, including: Girls and young women Youth living in poverty Young people with disabilities Migrants and displaced youth Youth affected by conflicts Youth impacted by climate disasters and emergencies GenU brings together: Governments United Nations agencies Private sector organizations Academic institutions Civil society organizations Youth-led groups Investors Development partners The partnership works to identify, scale, and support innovative youth-centered solutions globally. UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 Overview Internship Details Category Details Organization UNICEF Programme Generation Unlimited (GenU) Internship Type Full-time Remote Internship Duration 26 weeks Number of Positions 2 Location Remote / New York HQ Functional Areas Communication, Partnerships, Fundraising Application Deadline 08 June 2026 Available Internship Positions UNICEF is offering internship opportunities across two major hubs within Generation Unlimited. 1. Youth Engagement and Entrepreneurship Hub This position focuses on youth participation, entrepreneurship programming, and youth-centered innovation initiatives. Intern responsibilities include: Youth Engagement Supporting meaningful youth engagement strategies Assisting the Young People’s Action Team globally and regionally Supporting implementation of the Youth Centrality Strategy Gathering feedback from marginalized youth groups Helping integrate youth perspectives into programme design Entrepreneurship Programming Assisting entrepreneurship-focused initiatives Supporting mentorship sessions and workshops Helping young entrepreneurs build skills Facilitating connections with strategic partners Programme Support Supporting youth-led programme implementation Aligning projects with GenU strategic priorities Assisting entrepreneurship and youth empowerment programmes Advocacy and Outreach Developing blogs and digital advocacy materials Amplifying youth voices within GenU initiatives Supporting youth participation in advocacy forums 2. Global Solutions Hub This internship focuses on strategic planning, learning-to-earning initiatives, and knowledge-sharing activities. Responsibilities include: Global Solutions Portfolio Supporting resources for UNICEF Country Offices Assisting Learning-to-Earning programming initiatives Supporting GenU programme deployment globally Strategic Planning Assisting the development of GenU’s 2026–2029 strategic plan Supporting country consultations Drafting technical notes and planning documents Contributing to strategic reviews Knowledge Sharing Developing reports and blogs Supporting peer-to-peer learning initiatives Assisting webinars and thought leadership activities Supporting online learning platforms and global youth networks General Responsibilities of Interns All interns will also support broader operational and programmatic activities, including: Collaboration Working closely with GenU programme teams Coordinating with external partners Supporting integrated programme delivery Research and Analysis Conducting research on youth development trends Analyzing entrepreneurship and innovation issues Supporting evidence-based strategy development Administrative Support Organizing meetings Maintaining programme documentation Supporting logistical coordination The internship offers practical experience in: International development Programme management Youth engagement Policy support Global partnerships Advocacy Research Communication Benefits of the UNICEF Internship Selected interns will gain access to numerous professional development opportunities. Key Benefits Include: Hands-on international work experience Opportunity to work with UNICEF and global partners Exposure to international youth development initiatives Professional networking opportunities Experience in global programme implementation Flexible remote working arrangements Leave entitlement Reasonable accommodation support for persons with disabilities Exposure to high-level global policy discussions Skill development in research, advocacy, communication, and strategy The internship also provides a strong foundation for careers in: International development Public policy Youth advocacy Humanitarian work Social innovation Entrepreneurship support Global partnerships Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026, applicants must meet several academic and professional criteria. Applicants Must: Be enrolled in or have recently completed a graduate academic programme Study fields such as: International Relations Political Science Education Business Entrepreneurship Monitoring and Evaluation International Development Other related disciplines Applicants Should Also Have: Interest or experience in youth development and engagement Experience working with adolescents and youth programmes Understanding of humanitarian or development contexts Some volunteer or professional experience in youth-focused work Strong writing and communication skills Ability to prepare concept notes and pitch decks Preferred Skills and Qualifications UNICEF strongly prefers candidates with: Knowledge of research and qualitative analysis Experience with knowledge management Data visualization skills Experience participating in youth advisory bodies Interest in GenU programme areas Fluency in English is required. Knowledge of additional UN languages is considered an advantage, including: Arabic Chinese French Russian Spanish Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare and submit the following documents: Proof of university enrollment or recent graduation Official academic transcript Valid identification card Signed recommendation letter Proof of health insurance coverage for the internship duration UNICEF’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion UNICEF emphasizes diversity, equity, and inclusion throughout its recruitment process. The organization welcomes applicants regardless of: Race Ethnicity Gender identity Disability Nationality Religion Socio-economic background Sexual orientation UNICEF also maintains strict safeguarding and protection standards and conducts rigorous background checks on selected candidates. Important Internship Notes Applicants should note the following: The internship does not guarantee future employment with UNICEF. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Health insurance is mandatory. UNICEF may withdraw internship offers if required documentation is not provided. Selected candidates must comply with UNICEF safeguarding policies. Application Deadline Deadline: 08 June 2026 (Eastern Daylight Time) Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early before the deadline. How to Apply Applicants can learn more and apply through the official UNICEF careers platform Apply Here For more information on this internship visit here Discover more internship opportunities on OFY, click here The post UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 in New York (Remote): Fully Remote International Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates
summary: The World Intellectual Property Organization Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland is now open for applications. This highly competitive United Nations internship offers an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates in law, ethics, public international law, compliance, and related fields to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organizations. Hosted at WIPO headquarters in Geneva, the internship provides successful candidates with practical exposure to ethics policy development, international civil service law, legal research, compliance frameworks, and ethics advisory work within the UN system. The internship is expected to begin on October 1, 2026, with an initial duration of six months and the possibility of renewal up to a maximum of twelve months. The application deadline is June 19, 2026. WIPO is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to promoting innovation and protecting intellectual property globally. Through this internship, selected interns will work directly within the Ethics Office under the supervision of the Chief Ethics Officer and contribute to maintaining integrity, ethical conduct, and accountability across the organization. This opportunity is ideal for aspiring international lawyers, ethics professionals, compliance officers, policy researchers, and individuals interested in careers within the United Nations system. About the WIPO Ethics Office Internship The WIPO Ethics Office Internship Program is designed to help interns: Complement their academic studies with practical international experience Develop legal, analytical, and policy-related skills Gain exposure to ethics and integrity systems within the United Nations Understand international civil service law and governance Build professional networks within international organizations The internship is based entirely on-site in Geneva, Switzerland, and cannot be completed remotely. Interns will work within the Ethics Office under the Sector of the Director General. The Ethics Office plays a crucial role in ensuring that all WIPO personnel uphold the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and ethical conduct according to: The WIPO Convention WIPO Staff Regulations and Rules The WIPO Code of Ethics Standards of Conduct for the International Civil Service The office also provides confidential ethics advice, develops ethics-related policies, conducts awareness campaigns, and protects personnel from retaliation or reprisals. Internship Location and Duration Internship Location Geneva, Switzerland Expected Start Date October 1, 2026 Internship Duration Initial duration of 6 months Renewable up to 12 months maximum Working Arrangement Full-time 40 hours per week On-site internship at WIPO headquarters Financial Benefits and Internship Support Selected interns will receive several benefits during the internship period. Monthly Stipend Interns will receive: 2,170 Swiss francs per month Additional Benefits The internship package also includes: Medical insurance Annual leave Sick leave benefits Technical supervision and mentorship Professional development within the UN system This stipend is intended for full-time participation in the internship program. What Interns Will Do Interns will support the Ethics Office in legal, policy, communication, and research-related tasks. Main Responsibilities Include 1. Ethics and Policy Research Conduct research for new or revised ethics policies Analyze ethics-related frameworks within the UN system Support comparative legal and policy studies 2. Legal Research and Fact-Finding Collect information and documentation related to ethics cases Conduct jurisprudence and international civil service law research Assist with legal analysis and policy interpretation 3. Ethics Advisory Support Assist in preparing ethics-related guidance Support the development of confidential advisory materials 4. Communication and Awareness Activities Contribute to ethics outreach campaigns Help prepare educational materials and presentations Assist in awareness-raising initiatives 5. Documentation and Administrative Tasks Draft memoranda, reports, and correspondence Prepare PowerPoint presentations Support updates to Ethics Office web pages and intranet content 6. Additional Tasks Perform other related duties assigned by the Chief Ethics Officer Eligibility Criteria WIPO specifies that this internship falls under Category II internships. Applicants must belong to one of the following categories: Eligible Applicants Include Individuals pursuing their first advanced university degree who have completed coursework but not their final thesis Individuals who already hold one advanced degree and are pursuing another university degree Individuals who obtained their most recent advanced university degree less than two years before applying Educational Requirements Essential Requirement Applicants must possess: An advanced degree in law (Master’s level preferred) Preferred academic specializations include: Public international law Organizational ethics Compliance International civil service law Desirable Qualification Specialization in international civil service law Preferred Experience While not mandatory, WIPO values applicants with: Previous experience in international organizations Policy research experience Experience developing communication materials Familiarity with ethics or compliance systems Language Requirements Essential Excellent written and spoken English Desirable Very good knowledge of French Skills and Competencies Required WIPO is seeking candidates with strong professional and interpersonal skills. Key Competencies Include Excellent analytical abilities Strong legal drafting skills Effective written and verbal communication Teamwork and flexibility Integrity and professionalism Sound judgment Confidentiality handling skills Strong organizational capacity Ability to work with sensitive information Technical Skills Applicants should also be proficient in: Microsoft Office applications Zoom Digital collaboration tools Online communication systems Organizational Competencies Interns are expected to demonstrate: Effective communication Integrity Service orientation Innovation and adaptability Team spirit Respect for diversity Result-oriented performance Continuous learning Why This Internship Is Important This WIPO internship is a major opportunity for individuals seeking careers in: International law Human rights Ethics and compliance UN agencies International governance Public policy Diplomacy Global institutions Interns will gain first-hand exposure to how ethics systems function inside a major international organization and develop practical experience that can significantly strengthen future career prospects. How to Apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 Applicants must submit the following documents: Required Documents Motivation letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Submission Instructions Applications should be sent by email to: erecruit@wipo.int Required Subject Line Ethics Internship position 2026 Important Note For LinkedIn Easy Apply submissions: Combine both the CV and cover letter into one PDF file Applications without both documents will not be considered. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 is: June 19, 2026 Candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Applicants can learn more about the internship program on here Discover more internship opportunities on OFY here The post WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050
summary: Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050. Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures , this international initiative encourages people everywhere to use the power of imagination and storytelling to envision solutions to today’s social and environmental challenges. The competition is open globally with no age restrictions and no geographical limitations, making it one of the most inclusive international creative writing opportunities currently available. Participants stand a chance to win a grand prize of USD 10,000 while contributing to conversations about the future of humanity, sustainability, innovation, and social transformation. What is the Social Fiction Design Competition? The Social Fiction Design Competition is an international writing competition focused on “Social Fiction” — a form of imaginative storytelling similar to science fiction, but centered on social innovation and human progress rather than futuristic technology alone. The competition invites participants to imagine: What the world could look like in 2050 How young people may live in the future How societies can overcome inequality and injustice What sustainable communities might become How governments, businesses, and communities can work together for positive change The goal is to inspire new ways of thinking about social innovation and encourage people to imagine the world they truly want to build. According to Nobel Peace Laureate Muhammad Yunus : “If we do not imagine, it will not be done. If we imagine, someday it will happen.” This powerful idea forms the foundation of the competition. What is Social Fiction? Social Fiction is a storytelling approach that imagines a future society shaped by positive social transformation. Unlike traditional science fiction, which often focuses heavily on futuristic machines or scientific advancement, Social Fiction focuses on: Human-centered innovation Social justice Sustainability Equality Community transformation Ethical development Inclusive economies Environmental healing The concept encourages writers to creatively explore solutions to real-world problems and imagine societies where communities thrive. The organizers believe that imagination can inspire real-world innovation and policy change. Competition Theme: The Life of a Young Person in 2050 Participants are asked to imagine and describe: “How do you wish to see the life of a young person in the year 2050?” Writers are encouraged to take readers on a journey into a future world that is free from the social and environmental injustices affecting communities today. The competition emphasizes optimism, innovation, and bold thinking. Applicants should imagine a future where systems, institutions, and communities have evolved to create better opportunities and improved quality of life for younger generations. Writing Requirements Participants must submit an original Social Fiction piece between: Minimum: 1,000 words Maximum: 5,000 words The writing should creatively explore a fictional world in 2050 while addressing one or more major social or environmental issues. Suggested Focus Areas Participants may choose to focus on one or several of the following areas: Climate Change Agriculture Technology and Innovation Micro-credit Transport Literature Music Waste Management Circular Economy Tourism WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene) Employment Sports Health and Well-being Community Development Sustainability Social Equity The organizers encourage participants to think broadly and creatively about how these sectors could evolve positively by 2050. What Should Your Story Include? The competition encourages writers to explain how different stakeholders contribute to building a better future. Stories may include the role of: Governments Educational institutions Corporate organizations Local businesses Community leaders Entrepreneurs Youth movements Civil society organizations Participants should demonstrate how collaboration and innovation help shape a more just and sustainable society. Why This Competition Matters The Social Fiction Design Competition is not just a writing contest. It is a platform for global imagination and social innovation. At a time when the world faces challenges such as: Climate change Rising inequality Unemployment Environmental degradation Mental health concerns Economic instability Social exclusion The competition seeks bold visions of hope and possibility. It encourages people to move beyond criticism and begin imagining actionable futures worth building. Prize Opportunity: Win USD 10,000 One of the major attractions of the competition is the opportunity to win: USD 10,000 The prize recognizes outstanding creativity, originality, optimism, and social imagination. Beyond financial rewards, participants gain international exposure and become part of a global conversation on innovation and sustainable futures. Who Can Participate? One of the strongest features of the Social Fiction Design Competition is its accessibility. There are: No age restrictions No geographical limitations This means anyone from anywhere in the world can participate. Students, writers, professionals, activists, researchers, entrepreneurs, and aspiring storytellers are all encouraged to apply. Can You Apply More Than Once? According to the competition FAQ: Multiple applications are not permitted. Applicants are encouraged to focus on submitting one strong and well-developed Social Fiction piece. Is There an Application Fee? The competition states that there is: No application fee Participation is completely free. Can Teams Apply? The FAQ section indicates that applicants should review the application guidelines regarding team participation. Participants are encouraged to verify current submission rules through the official competition platform. Previous Winners and Finalists The competition has already attracted talented participants from different countries around the world. Previous Winner Amit Kumar Mishra – Nepal Previous Finalists Neha Sabu – India Wan Faizal Mohd – Malaysia Anne Julienne Barcelona – United States Tyfur Rahman – Bangladesh The diversity of past finalists demonstrates the truly international nature of the competition. Why You Should Apply Participating in the Social Fiction Design Competition can help writers and changemakers: Showcase creativity globally Build visibility as a thought leader Contribute to social innovation conversations Inspire real-world solutions Develop storytelling and writing skills Gain recognition for visionary ideas Connect imagination with impact For young innovators, students, activists, and creative thinkers, this is a unique opportunity to influence future thinking through storytelling. How to Apply Applicants are required to: Write an original Social Fiction piece Ensure the submission falls within 1,000–5,000 words Complete the online application form Submit the story through the official competition platform For inquiries: Email: info@yy.ventures WhatsApp: +880 1648-397420 Apply Here For more information on this competition visit here Discover more opportunities on OFY here The post Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
